description: Source registry for the long-run Croatian GDP-per-capita series. The data itself is NOT committed (git-ignored under data/raw, data/processed, data/warehouse); only this registry, the transcribed Tica CSV, and rendered charts are. Runtime provenance (download dates, row counts) is regenerated into outputs/facts/gdp_provenance.json.

join_keys.note: Country code differs by provider: 'HRV' (Maddison, PWT, World Bank), 'HR' (Eurostat). Loaders normalize to year-level series for Croatia.

sources.name: Eurostat national accounts, real GDP per capita (ESA 2010)
Maddison Project Database 2023 (Bolt & van Zanden 2024)
sources.unit: real GDP per capita, 2011 international (PPP) dollars
sources.access: xlsx GET from Dataverse datafile 421302 (sheet 'Full data'); or CRAN package `MaddisonData`
sources.license: see MPD citation policy (cite source papers when showing <12 countries)
sources.citation: Bolt, J. & van Zanden, J.L. (2024). Maddison style estimates of the evolution of the world economy: A new 2023 update. Journal of Economic Surveys.

sources.name: Tica (2004), The Estimation of 1910-1989 Per Capita GDP in Croatia
sources.unit: per-capita GDP (academic reconstruction, own base; used for growth rates only)
sources.access: PDF only -- full paper transcribed into data/reference/Tica_2004_Croatia_GDP_data.xlsx (15 tables); the headline GVSM annual series (sheet GVSM_annual_series, gdppc_1990gk) is extracted into data/reference/tica_2004_gdppc.csv for the splice. War years 1914-1919 and 1940-1946 are blank in the source.
sources.citation: Tica, J. (2004). Zagreb International Review of Economics & Business 7(1):103-133.
sources.warning: Backcast estimates; author advises caution. Use for growth-rate chaining, not as published levels.

sources.name: Penn World Table 10.01
sources.unit: rgdpo/rgdpe in millions 2017 US$ -> per capita in 2017 US$
sources.access: CRAN package `pwt10` (data bundled in R library)

sources.name: World Bank WDI, GDP per capita (constant 2015 US$)

context_only_references.name: Vinski; Stajic (Nacionalni dohodak Jugoslavije 1923-1939)
context_only_references.use: interwar national-income foundations cited by Tica; print only

context_only_references.name: Nikolic (2017), New Economic History of Yugoslavia 1919-1939
Kukic, Socialist Growth Revisited: Insights from Yugoslavia
